talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center
talk_metadata.talk_title: RAS and RAF targeting in MSS metastatic colorectal cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we don't have that data formally yet"
Speaker said: "I don't think that MSS will be solved by a single drug, but it will be solved with strategy"
Speaker said: "we still don't have great ideas yet or at least great signals of what to do afterwards"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Emergence of KRAS, NRAS, MAP2K1 mutations and MET amplifications at progression
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker noted a "fairly high prevalence of KRAS, NRAS, MAP2K1 mutations, as well as some MET amplifications" acquired at progression without detailing why they dominate
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Some patients had multiple co‑occurring alterations yet still derived benefit, indicating a subset response

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: BRAF V600E inhibition with EGFR blockade plus chemotherapy backbone
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled all‑comer MSS BRAF V600E patients without stratifying by baseline ctDNA mutational signatures that predict secondary mutations
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Baseline high cell‑cycle transcriptional signature (predictive of secondary KRAS/NRAS mutations)
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial ctDNA monitoring in control arm to track emerging resistance
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Control arm powered for response rate but not for OS comparison across molecular sub‑groups

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Encorafenib (BRAF V600E inhibitor) limited to V600E mutation
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that non‑V600E BRAF alterations have limited therapeutic options, exposing a narrow chemical scope
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on combination with EGFR inhibitor and chemotherapy may dilute signal in heterogeneous MSS population

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed pre‑clinical data that EGFR inhibition alone is inactive in BRAF‑mutant CRC, yet concluded that adding EGFR to BRAF inhibition is sufficient for durable pathway inhibition without needing additional targets

key_data_gaps: No overall survival data for the EC‑only arm
Lack of biomarker‑stratified analysis for secondary resistance mutations
No head‑to‑head comparison with standard of care chemotherapy alone

crispro_opportunity.description: Baseline high cell‑cycle transcriptional signature predicts emergence of secondary KRAS/NRAS mutations; CrisPRO could develop a predictive model to select patients who need upfront combination with PI3K or MET inhibitors
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Those that have really high cell cycle signaling on transcriptomic profiling at baseline are the ones that are much more likely to have these secondary mutations"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's computational spine to integrate baseline RNA‑seq and ctDNA VAF data to generate a risk score for adaptive mutability

crispro_opportunity.description: Pre‑clinical data suggest PI3K‑RAS breaker (e.g., BridgeBio 10203) may overcome resistance mediated by PI3K pathway activation
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "The idea, of course, is that normally PI3 kinase, when it's activated through upstream RTKs, will signal down through AKT... we have a covalent inhibitor that prevents the binding of the mutated RAS"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model synergistic effects of KRAS/PI3K breaker combos to prioritize clinical trial designs

crispro_opportunity.description: YAP/TEAD activation identified as a universal resistance pathway after KRAS/RAF inhibition; targeting TEAD could restore sensitivity
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "The universal finding here, nearly universal, was that YAP signaling was indeed up"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Predict YAP/TEAD activation from transcriptomic signatures and propose TEAD inhibitor combos

cited_competitors.name: Vemurafenib
cited_competitors.context: first BRAF mutant‑selective inhibitor that failed to show activity in CRC

cited_competitors.name: Encorafenib
cited_competitors.context: BRAF inhibitor approved in combination with cetuximab and chemotherapy

cited_competitors.name: Sotorasib
cited_competitors.context: KRAS G12C inhibitor combined with panitumumab in CodeBreaK 300

cited_competitors.name: Adagrasib
cited_competitors.context: KRAS G12C inhibitor combined with cetuximab, response rate 34%

cited_competitors.name: Pluxerafenib
cited_competitors.context: Paradox‑breaker BRAF inhibitor with limited activity post‑encorafenib

cited_competitors.name: Amivantamab
cited_competitors.context: Bispecific MET/EGFR antibody discussed as potential partner

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal sequencing of BRAF/EGFR inhibition with chemotherapy to prevent cross‑resistance?
Can YAP/TEAD inhibition synergize with KRAS/RAF inhibitors in patients?
What is the durability of ctDNA clearance as a surrogate for long‑term benefit?
